title: Fun factor and tone evaluation
target_tone: quirky & mysterious — druide parlant, humour decale, secrets caches partout, narration a double fond

quirky_elements: Bug templates: 'Je bugue, donc je pense'
mysterious_elements: Celtic vocabulary: brume, pierre, ogham, nemeton, sidhe, dolmen, korrigans
Self-reference: 'Un vieux druide aux secrets rapioces'
Cryptic hints: 'Le cercle de pierres n'est pas un cercle'
merlin_persona_tone.humor_type: Dry, subtle, self-aware meta-humor disguised as druide wisdom
merlin_persona_tone.verdict: Persona nails the 'quirky & mysterious' target perfectly

scenario_tone_diversity.strength: Scenarios have strong narrative hooks and double meanings
scenario_tone_diversity.weakness: Tone variety limited to serious/solemn — needs more tonal range

example_double_meaning.twist: Child has been adopted by forest / child has become something else
example_double_meaning.tone_shift: innocent rescue → body horror / loss of humanity

scenario_tone_diversity.recommendation: Add 2-3 scenarios with 'absurde' or 'farceur' tone to balance seriousness

card_option_distinctness.reason: No autoplay logs available to analyze actual card option texts
card_option_distinctness.design_intent: 3 options per card — Left (safe), Center (balanced), Right (risky)

theoretical_assessment.mechanical_distinctness: ✅ OK (DC ranges create different success rates)

card_option_distinctness.target: Each option must be narratively distinct, not just mechanically

example_good_distinctness.right: Challenge the korrigan to a riddle contest (bold)

card_option_distinctness.recommendation: Collect 50 LLM-generated cards from autoplay and analyze option texts for variety

choice_meaningfulness.evidence: Scenario anchors create branching narratives based on flags

anchor_2_branches.if_flag_ruban_trouve: Chasseur remarque le ruban rouge — eclair de panique

example.impact: Player's earlier choice (finding ribbon) changes NPC reaction

choice_meaningfulness.assessment: Choices have narrative memory and consequence

variety_assessment.recommendation: Add 3-5 more scenarios with diverse tones to reach target variety

examples.secret: AI from future reliving memories (lore reveal at T4)
Child has been changed by forest / is no longer human
Player carries unfinished oath they don't remember

secrets_and_double_meanings.verdict: Secrets are LAYERED and DISCOVERABLE over multiple runs
secrets_and_double_meanings.lore_progression: T0 (surface) → T1 (patterns) → T2 (Merlin's nature) → T3 (cycles) → T4 (full truth)

quirky.evidence: Merlin's glitches, meta-humor, Celtic vocabulary quirks

mysterious.evidence: Layered secrets, cryptic hints, double meanings, lore reveals over 50+ runs

humour_decale.evidence: Present in Merlin persona (bugs, lapsus), MISSING in scenarios

narration_double_fond.evidence: Scenario twists, Merlin's true nature, child transformation, oath mystery

action_items.action: Collect 50 LLM-generated cards from autoplay logs
action_items.rationale: Verify option text distinctness and narrative variety
Balance serious scenarios with comedic relief
examples: Korrigan market — absurd haggling with faeries over meaningless objects
Drunk dolmen — talking stone that can't stop hiccuping prophecies
Merlin's lost hat — ridiculous fetch quest that reveals deep lore

action_items.rationale: Expand glitch templates for long-term replayability

strengths: Merlin persona is PERFECTLY quirky & mysterious
Secrets are layered across 50+ runs (meta-progression lore reveals)
Scenario twists create double meanings (child transformation, oath mystery)
Celtic vocabulary creates strong atmospheric tone
weaknesses: Scenario tone variety limited to serious/solemn (no comedy)
No autoplay data to verify LLM-generated card option distinctness
Only 2 scenarios in catalogue (need 5-10 for variety)
overall_verdict: Tone is STRONG for mystery and secrets, MODERATE for humor variety. Add comedic scenarios to fully achieve 'quirky & mysterious' balance.
